displayTitle: Null First, Then Spend the Gain

beats.narration: A fatigue crack shifts the coil voltage by a fraction of one percent. To reach the screen, that whisper needs amplifying a hundred thousand times.

payload.title: GAIN MOVES MOUNTAINS
payload.sub: Microvolt changes ride a carrier hundreds of times larger.

beats.narration: But never amplify the carrier itself. Null the standing signal first, so the amplifier spends its whole range on the flaw, not the steady voltage.

tiles.s: Volts of carrier you don't need

beats.narration: Gain is a multiplier, written in decibels. Burn in the anchor: plus six decibels doubles an unclipped signal. That doubling is your field linearity check.
Push past the linear range and peaks clip. Watch for it: flat tops, signals pinned at the edge, a reference that won't double on plus six.
Gain makes signals bigger, never cleaner. When it saturates, the fix never changes: reduce gain, re-null, then re-standardize.

payload.sub: Bigger is not cleaner — SNR is won elsewhere.
